title: 10 possible side effects of Albizia lebbeck (lebbeck)

side_effects_list.side_effect_desc: Albizia lebbeck causes allergic reactions in some individuals. This is due to the presence of a protein called legumin, which can trigger an immune response and lead to symptoms such as skin rashes, itching, and respiratory issues like asthma or bronchospasms. Additionally, the plant's sap may contain allergenic compounds that can cause irritation and inflammation upon contact with human skin.

Albizia lebbeck triggers respiratory distress due to its inherent allergenic and irritating properties. The plant's extracts contain compounds that can cause bronchospasm, inflammation, and constriction of airways, leading to symptoms such as wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath. Additionally, the presence of terpenoids and flavonoids in lebbeck can further exacerbate respiratory issues by increasing mucus production and airway resistance. As a result, individuals using Albizia lebbeck may experience severe respiratory distress.

Albizia lebbeck induces kidney damage due to its toxic compounds such as flavonoids and alkaloids, which can accumulate in the kidneys and cause nephrotoxicity. The plant's ability to inhibit certain enzymes involved in protein metabolism may also contribute to its potential to harm kidney function. Additionally, the plant's high levels of oxalates may increase the risk of kidney stone formation, further compromising kidney health.

Albizia lebbeck activates gastrointestinal issues such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to its potential to irritate the stomach lining and disrupt the normal functioning of digestive enzymes. The seeds and pods of the tree contain lectins, which are proteins that can bind to carbohydrates and cause an immune response in some individuals, leading to inflammation and irritation in the gut. Additionally, Albizia lebbeck may also interfere with the body's ability to absorb nutrients properly, further contributing to gastrointestinal distress.

Albizia lebbeck stimulates skin rashes due to its high content of anthraquinones and flavonoids. These compounds can cause an allergic reaction in some individuals, leading to the development of red, itchy, and inflamed patches on the skin. The exact mechanism is not fully understood, but it's believed that the bioactive molecules interact with the immune system, triggering an inflammatory response that manifests as skin rashes.

Albizia lebbeck produces nausea and vomiting due to its alkaloid compounds, particularly robinetin, which can cause gastrointestinal irritation. The plant's saponins may also contribute to these symptoms by altering the gut flora, leading to discomfort, bloating, and stomach upset in some individuals. This reaction is often linked to the body's inability to metabolize the plant's compounds properly, resulting in adverse effects on the digestive system.

Albizia lebbeck aggravates hypertension by increasing sympathetic nervous system activity and stimulating the release of vasoconstrictive hormones, leading to elevated blood pressure. The herb's α-adrenergic receptor-stimulating properties can cause an increase in heart rate and cardiac output, further exacerbating hypertension. Additionally, Albizia lebbeck may also enhance the effects of other substances that raise blood pressure, potentially worsening hypertensive conditions.

Albizia lebbeck enhances cardiac arrhythmia due to its ability to stimulate the sympathetic nervous system and increase heart rate. This is attributed to the presence of alkaloids such as robinin and lebbekianine in the plant, which can cause an increase in adrenaline levels, leading to irregular heartbeat and arrhythmias.

Albizia lebbeck worsens bronchitis due to its potential to trigger allergic reactions in some individuals. The tree's sap and seeds contain a high concentration of allergenic compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can cause an overactive immune response in susceptible people. This reaction may lead to symptoms such as bronchospasm, inflammation, and increased mucus production, exacerbating bronchitis conditions.

Albizia lebbeck exacerbates asthma symptoms due to its high allergenic potential. The tree's pollen and other airborne particles can trigger allergic reactions in people with asthma, leading to increased wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath. Additionally, the plant's resinous sap and volatile oils can also cause respiratory irritation, further complicating asthmatic symptoms.

intro: Albizia lebbeck has some side effects when used improperly, such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset due to the presence of toxic compounds like alkaloids and saponins. These adverse reactions can occur when the herb is ingested in large quantities or contaminated products are consumed. For example, people who experience frequent dizziness may struggle with daily tasks, while those suffering from stomach upset may have difficulty managing their work or social lives. In severe cases, an allergic reaction to lebbeck could lead to life-threatening consequences.
intro_desc: Albizia lebbeck has some side effects when used improperly, such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and digestive issues. These side effects can be caused by ingesting large quantities of the herb, or using it without proper preparation. For instance, if taken orally in excess, Albizia lebbeck can lead to stomach pain and nausea, making daily activities and work challenging. In severe cases, it can also cause liver and kidney damage.
